name: Conor Lamb
subject: A new voice
body:
Friend --

I won't waste much of your time making you read an email. I'd much rather ask if you could take a few minutes to watch our new video -- one that I think gives a good look at the kind of leader I want to be for our district in Congress.

**[Friend, please take a few minutes to watch this video -- and if you're as excited about bringing new leadership to Congress as I am, then I hope I can count on you to chip in $5 or more to help me win on March 13th!](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/ekX1KXzfV-HkP3GZGH_yww==?utm_campaign=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Lamb&ms=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM)**

| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/ekX1KXzfV-HkP3GZGH_yww==?utm_campaign=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Lamb&ms=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM)
---

[**WATCH >> DONATE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/ekX1KXzfV-HkP3GZGH_yww==?utm_campaign=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Lamb&ms=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM)
---
As I've been out knocking doors and talking to people across this district, a lot of people tell me they don't believe our government can protect people anymore. I'm running for Congress because we can't let that be true. When I hear that as a Marine officer, those are marching orders.

I know that with real leadership in Congress, we can get back to doing what's right for hardworking men and women in Southwestern Pennsylvania. We can protect Social Security and Medicare, make healthcare more affordable, bring back good jobs by rebuilding our crumbling infrastructure, and finally take meaningful action to fight the heroin epidemic that has devastated too many families in our district.

**[So take a minute to watch this new video and hear more from me about the need for new leaders in Washington, then chip in $5 or more to help make sure I can get there to fight for you!](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/ekX1KXzfV-HkP3GZGH_yww==?utm_campaign=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Lamb&ms=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM)**

Best,

Conor

[**DONATE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/ekX1KXzfV-HkP3GZGH_yww==?utm_campaign=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Lamb&ms=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180206_Video_fnd_xq___KM)
---
Paid for Conor Lamb for Congress
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/5IzKJ4RxDX7bnxgCNr1eqA==/lsMg4hrON5uVGccqhF_oKQ==).

Conor Lamb for Congress
PO BOX 10381
Pittsburgh, PA 15234

Getting tough on MS-13

Elizabeth Alvarado, Robert Mickens, Evelyn Rodriguez, and Freddy Cuevas joined President Donald J. Trump last week as four of the special guests for his first State of the Union Address. They are the parents of Nisa Mickens and Kayla Cuevas, two young girls who were killed by the gang MS-13 in September 2016.

It’s estimated that MS-13 has as many as 10,000 members in at least 40 states. In the immigration framework President Trump sent to Congress last month, the White House calls for a bill that will give law enforcement the tools it needs to combat this brutal gang and stop its proliferation into American communities.

Today, the President is hosting a roundtable with law enforcement professionals and community leaders to discuss what immigration reforms are needed to dismantle this transnational gang once and for all.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c99e0090bfad9f5ec0eb432d039b512f5345cfa823e24ffd3f0e56e9a9258f1f970245d65afbaf4abcf52d514813758b8baa1344ca2073
Watch as President Trump hosts the roundtable at 1:45 PM ET.

Vice President Pence’s message in South Korea

Vice President Mike Pence left yesterday en route to Asia, where he’ll lead the American Presidential Delegation at the 2018 Olympic Winter Games in South Korea. Second Lady Karen Pence joined him as a member of the Delegation.

This year’s Games hold particular importance given the recent actions of North Korea related to its nuclear weapons program. President Trump held a call Friday with South Korean President Moon Jae-in before meeting with defectors from North Korea in the Oval Office.

It was announced over the weekend that a special guest will accompany Vice President Pence. “Honored that Fred Warmbier, father of Otto Warmbier, will join us” in South Korea, the Vice President tweeted. Otto Warmbier was an American student detained in North Korea for 17 months before being released in severe condition and passing away four days later.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c99e0090bfad9f5a647a3776451db6d9081950eead9e4fd46bf90af7093e2849af34e4a977f59b059b9756304f1429cff85da3c2c12b21
See the Vice President’s tweet: “We pledge to honor Otto’s memory with American resolve.”

‘Four magnificent words’

President Trump addressed workers at an Ohio manufacturing company yesterday, talking through the effects of tax reform and previewing what the Administration will fight for in 2018.

"Right here in Cincinnati, the Sheffer Corporation announced every single worker was getting a $1,000 tax cut bonus,” the President said. “That’s good. Hard working patriotic Americans like you are what makes this country run.”

At heart, tax relief is about reinvigorating the great middle class that built America. “We’re bringing back those four magnificent words: ‘Made in the USA,’” the President said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c99e0090bfad9fbe4573955d40aac220df7d67eaafa90657fce29843e40d212860ab1e45e22b4155da140402179a5f05e72e2e3487214e
Read President Trump’s message: “Thank you for believing in the American worker.”

Bonus read:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=09c99e0090bfad9f82aa77892f68e2e7e0e1978572f194f52f942edd12ace2e4e386cfa03ae0048a807d8061231547576893ffa9f85df0e1
President Trump achieved the biggest tax cuts in American history

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump at Cincinnati Children’s Hospital | February 5, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will host a law enforcement roundtable on MS-13. Later, the President will sign a National Security Presidential Memorandum establishing the National Vetting Center.

This morning, Vice President Mike Pence and the Second Lady arrived in Tokyo, Japan.

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Moving Forward in the New Year
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501132762%2501Moving%2bForward%2bin%2bthe%2bNew%2bYear%250114174.7932059.3804209.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501132762%2501Moving%2bForward%2bin%2bthe%2bNew%2bYear%250114174.7932059.3804209.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **February 6, 2018** |
---|---|---|---
| | | In last week's State of the Union address, the President identified a number of issues that I have been eager to tackle for some time, including modernizing our infrastructure, reducing the skyrocketing costs of prescription drugs, and devoting real resources to counter the opioid epidemic. In fact, in each of these areas, I have joined with my Democratic colleagues to present specific proposals. To date, neither the President nor my Republican colleagues have supported these proposals. I hope that will change this year. Chris Van Hollen
---
### The Path Forward
Once again, Congress faces a deadline to resolve the issue of funding the federal government. Last month, we were able to work out a short-term agreement to end the shutdown and resume negotiations. But budgeting three weeks at a time is no way to run a business, much less a government. We must provide for the vital functions of the government--from defending our country; to continuing life-saving medical research at NIH; to supporting kids in Head Start, seniors, and veterans. We must also provide sufficient funding for community health centers and fighting the opioid epidemic, and ensure that our Dreamers have a secure future. In addition to these national priorities, Maryland relies on stable federal funding for projects ranging from Metro improvements to the Chesapeake Bay cleanup. We have a lot to get done this week--but I'm meeting with colleagues on both sides of the aisle, and I am determined to move forward on these issues on a bipartisan basis.
### What the Tax Bill Means for Marylanders
As a result of the tax plan that President Trump and Congressional Republicans passed, the Maryland Board of Revenue Estimates calculates that 376,000 Maryland households will be hit with federal tax increases--including 123,000 Maryland families with incomes between $25,000 and $50,000. This is unacceptable. We must protect Marylanders from these staggering impacts. And we must hold the Trump Administration responsible for the fact that hundreds of thousands of Maryland families are paying higher taxes while millionaires, billionaires, and big corporations get a huge windfall. I will continue pushing for solutions on both the state and federal level that mitigate these impacts on Maryland families.
### Protecting Our Environment
Last week, I was proud to reintroduce legislation on an issue that is crucial to our future as a nation--addressing climate change. The Healthy Climate and Family Security Act is a 'cap and dividend' plan that would use a market-based approach to place a price on carbon pollution and return the proceeds directly to hardworking Americans, increasing their spending power and growing our entire economy. In fact, a University of Massachusetts Amherst study found a 'cap and divided' approach would mean more than 80 percent of families would see more money in their pockets, even before taking into account the economic benefits of preventing the costly impacts from extreme weather events, rising sea levels, and other disruptions. Also last week, in my role as a Member on the Environment & Public Works Committee, I had the opportunity to question EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt. The EPA funds programs that directly impact the cleanup of the Chesapeake Bay, and I raised concerns about actions Mr. Pruitt has recently taken that could have a negative impact on those efforts. As your Senator, I'll continue to fight to protect the Bay and to make sure the Administration is held accountable on these issues.
### Deterring Foreign Interference in Our Elections
As we saw in the 2016 elections, our democratic process is vulnerable to attacks by hostile foreign powers--and we expect the threat will grow in future years. The United States must do everything possible to prevent these efforts to undermine our elections. That's why I introduced bipartisan legislation with Senator Marco Rubio (R-Fl.) called the Defending Elections from Threats by Establishing Redlines (DETER) Act. It would send a powerful message to Russia and any other foreign actors seeking to disrupt our elections: if you attack American candidates, campaigns, or voting infrastructure, you will automatically trigger severe sanctions and economic penalties. I'm working with both Democrats and Republicans to build support to pass it without delay. You can find out more information about this [legislation here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontent%2fvan-hollen-rubio-introduce-legislation-deter-foreign-interference-american-elections&redir_log=916944313961887).
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14174.7932059.3804209.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)
|
---

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: DeVos Op-Ed (The New York Times, 2017)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
Around this time last year, I penned an Op-Ed for _The New York Times_ about the main reasons I voted "No" to confirm Betsy DeVos as Secretary of Education.
Here's an excerpt:
_Our nation recognized early in its history that public education is a necessary foundation for a democracy. It's critical that we continue to support a strong public education system that prepares our young people, all of them, to participate in our democracy and compete on a fair footing in the work force._
_For this reason, our public officials should share a reverence for the importance of public education to our country's success, both now and in the future. And they must show a commitment to enforcing our laws so that all students have the opportunity to succeed._
_That is why I oppose President Trump's nominee for secretary of education, Betsy DeVos._
Of course, you know that DeVos was ultimately confirmed.
In her first year as Secretary of Education, she has tried to jam through enormous funding cuts for our public schools, failed to stand up against discrimination and sexual harassment in our education system, and blocked policies that could help make student loan debt more manageable.
**I 'm fighting every day to block DeVos's discriminatory education policies, but I need to know that you're by my side. [Will you please join me and sign my petition calling on Betsy DeVos to protect our students?](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfcc/2a380d31/7c20880/f1d446/3770597073/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/devosoneyear/?code=hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_devos-1-year")**
Thank you for standing up for what is right.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan
[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfcc/2a380d31/7c20880/f1d447/3770597073/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOF9kZXZvcy0xLXllYXIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE4MDExOF9GdW5kaW5nQmlsbF9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018_devos-1-year?refcode=e20180118_FundingBill_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

name: Maya Cantrell, Democrats.org
subject: Can I text you, Derek?
body:
You can receive the most important updates from the Democratic Party. Sign up now:
---
Hi, Derek -- I'm Maya. I work on the Mobilization team here at the DNC, and I've been tasked with building out our text messaging program. I'm so excited to get started, and I'd love it if you joined -- **sign up to get text updates from the Democratic Party now:**

**[ SIGN UP TO
GET TEXTS](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522118/4067b0a/5497f9a91/7e524b41/2392543972/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/get-texts-from-the-Democratic-Party?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+SIGN+UP+TOGET+TEXTS&utm_campaign=em_20180206_mc_mc&source=em_20180206_mc_mc&refcode=em_20180206_mc_mc")
** Text messaging is one of the best ways to stay in touch with supporters like you -- it's quick, it's timely, and it's personal.

We're going to use our SMS program to share breaking news, calls to action, new tools, shareable videos, and so much more.

Yeah, we'll ask you to chip in a few dollars at key moments, but text messaging isn't just a fundraising tool -- it's a great opportunity to help Democrats organize at the local level. You'll have the chance to stay in touch with the Democratic Party like never before, and I can't wait to get started.

**[Now that we're in a critical election year, it's important to stay engaged. Sign up now to receive text message updates.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522118/4067b0a/5497f9a91/7e524b4e/2392543972/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/get-texts-from-the-Democratic-Party?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Now+that+were+in+a+critical+election+yea&utm_campaign=em_20180206_mc_mc&source=em_20180206_mc_mc&refcode=em_20180206_mc_mc")**

Looking forward to being in touch with you soon, Derek!

Maya

Maya Cantrell
Democratic National Committee

Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522118/4067b0a/5497f9a91/7e524b4c/2392543972/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180206_mc_mc&source=em_20180206_mc_mc&refcode=em_20180206_mc_mc"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Thanks for supporting our party.

subject: Coastal drilling is not a game

Hello,

You might not have seen Andrew's email on Super Bowl Sunday, but let's be clear -- coastal drilling is not a game.

Donald Trump is putting 90% of our ocean's acreage at risk and Governor Rick Scott is playing politics with our shores. Future generations may never get to spend an afternoon on Florida's beautiful beaches without an oil rig in sight, and coastal towns' economies will suffer.

This is a direct attack on our environment and the Department of Interior is taking public comments -- but not for much longer.

[**This is urgent: It 's up to us to stand up and speak out. Add your name now and demand our waters are protected.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31dc/9e00690/43fc01a8/2403855b/977761527/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/no-coastal-drilling/gillum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+This+is+urgent+Itrsquos+up+to+us+to+stan&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")

Thank you,

Team Gillum

\----Begin Forwarded Message----

From: Andrew Gillum
Subject: Surprise! Governor Scott Lied
Date: Sunday, February 4, 2018

Hello --

For spending so much time in Florida, Donald Trump doesn't seem too worried about ruining the Sunshine State.

Last month, Trump's Administration announced plans to auction off drilling rights for 90% of our nation's ocean acreage, making the Pacific, Atlantic, and Gulf coasts, as well as the Arctic, available for development.

This is a direct attack on coastal states like ours -- but we have a chance to stop it.

**[The Department of the Interior has recently opened a public comment period, so add your name to demand they protect our waters.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31dc/9e00690/43fc01a8/2403855d/977761527/VEsC/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/no-coastal-drilling/gillum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=4+-+The+Department+of+the+Interior+has+recen&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

Our coastlines are among the most majestic places on Earth -- yet Trump's administration is turning over these areas to the world's biggest polluters. This will not only devastate marine life and threaten our beaches, but jeopardize the economies of coastal communities.

And while Governor Rick Scott promised to protect Florida's coastlines and stand up to Trump's administration, he ultimately went back on his promise to further his own political ambitions -- while Floridians pay the price.

**[We can not let Trump and Scott sell our environment for short-term profits. Add your name now and make your voice heard.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31dc/9e00690/43fc01a8/2403855c/977761527/VEsD/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/no-coastal-drilling/gillum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=5+-+We+can+not+let+Trump+and+Scott+sell+our+&utm_campaign=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em18_180204_Drill1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

Thank you,

Andrew Gillum

Hello,

First off, thank you for joining the team.

Here at Team Gillum, we know it's possible to have a major impact on a campaign and on an election -- even if you aren't a billionaire or ultra well-connected lobbyist. We know that the only way we can truly rebuild Florida into a state that works for everyone is if _everyone_ is included in the process.

After witnessing Doug Jones' historic win in the Alabama U.S. Senate special election, it's more clear than ever. Black voters, especially Black women, played a major role in his victory. When people of all different races, ethnicities, and backgrounds come together, they will win.

**[And that's exactly what we're doing here at Team Gillum. If you're with us, follow Andrew on Facebook to join the fight.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31df/9e00690/43cd3038/2403856b/3979050663/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/andrewgillumfl/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+And+thats+exactly+what+were+doing+here+a&utm_campaign=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG&source=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG")**

It's going to take a lot of power, hope, and determination to rebuild Florida after Governor Rick Scott's eight years in office. But after the momentum we've seen since launching this campaign, we're more confident than ever that we can do it. Together.

It's critical that everyone exercise their right to vote and make their voice heard, even when some try so hard to stop you. Everyone, no matter your background, can make a difference and impact -- and Andrew will need you on Election Day.

**[When people work together, anything is possible. So join Team Gillum and stay up-to-date with the campaign by liking us on Facebook now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31df/9e00690/43cd3038/2403856a/3979050663/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/andrewgillumfl/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+When+people+work+together+anything+is+po&utm_campaign=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG&source=em17_180105_Welc2Recur_engg_gq_ful__TG")**

Thank you,

Team Gillum

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Holcomb=20for=20Indiana?=
subject: ICYMI: HIP is here to stay
body:
Last week, Governor Eric Holcomb joined U.S. Health & Human Services Secretary Alex Azar (a fellow Hoosier!) to announce that Indiana has received... | | | | [View this email in your browser](http://mailchi.mp/bdacf7d8ccf2/icymi-hip-is-here-to-stay?e=716de7d363)
---
| | |
---
| **Governor Holcomb Announces Federal Approval for Enhanced Healthy Indiana Plan** New Plan Helps Indiana Attack the Opioid Epidemic & Connects Hoosiers to Job Training Services
---
|
---
| | **"HIP is more than just a health care coverage plan for those in need. It's __our__ plan."**
-Governor Eric Holcomb
---
|
---
| Last week, Governor Eric Holcomb joined U.S. Health & Human Services Secretary Alex Azar (_a fellow Hoosier!_) to announce that Indiana has received federal approval to enhance the Healthy Indiana Plan (HIP)!

HIP, which is Indiana's **consumer-driven alternative to traditional Medicaid** , provides health coverage to more than _400,000 Hoosier adults_. And when it comes to solving our nation's largest health care coverage problems, Indiana is developing _innovative, state-driven solutions._

A key part of Indiana's new federal Medicaid waiver includes **funding dedicated to the opioid crisis** and addiction treatment, as well as resources to help connect more Hoosiers on HIP with **employment training and services**.
---
|
---
| | **"HIP has become the gold standard for state-****administered**** health care programs, but we're not going to rest on our laurels. There's more work to be done to improve the health of Hoosiers all across our state. I have said before -- we did not come this far to **_only come this far_**."**
-Governor Eric Holcomb
---
|
---
| _**Key Details on Enhancing HIP:**_** **
* **Improving Hoosier Lives:** Since its launch in 2007 by former-Governor Mitch Daniels and through its expansion by former-Governor Mike Pence in 2015, HIP has grown and provided patient-driven health coverage options for low-income Hoosiers.
* **Attacking the Opioid Epidemic:** Indiana will receive up to $80 million annually in funding to provide addiction treatment services for HIP and Medicaid members.
* **Connecting Hoosiers to Job Training:** Indiana has about 95,000 job openings throughout the state. As part of updates to Indiana's waiver, Indiana will expand its existing Gateway to Work initiative, connecting HIP members to job training, education and community engagement activities.

---
| ["IT'S OUR PLAN": Watch the Video](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=44ff520ffb&e=716de7d363 ""IT'S OUR PLAN": Watch the Video")
---
|
---
| | | _**What They're Saying:**_
"Today Hoosiers like Governor Holcomb and Secretary Azar are leading the nation when it comes to developing innovative, state-driven solutions to our nation's biggest health care problems. Following months of collaboration, __Hoosiers will directly see the rewards for this hard work__ \-- with a huge influx of resources coming to Indiana to expand treatment for opioid addiction and connect Hoosiers with job training opportunities. At its core, __great government service is about helping improve lives for all people__ , and today's great news from Governor Holcomb and President Trump's health care team is a big win for improving the livelihood of Hoosiers throughout our state."
**-Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer**

"Today’s announcement is one significant step in __a long legacy of innovation in person-centered health care__. Indiana’s vision and ours goes beyond the provision of quality health care. It recognizes that Medicaid can become a pathway out of poverty.”
**-Alex Azar, Health and Human Services Secretary**

“A heartfelt congratulations and thank you to Gov. Holcomb and his team on obtaining an extension for our Healthy Indiana Plan from the federal government. HIP has been a great success, providing health care for 400,000 low-income Hoosiers, and __I’m pleased that this consumer-driven program will continue to operate here in Indiana__."
**-Indiana Senate President Pro Tem David Long**

“This waiver extension of the Healthy Indiana Plan is tremendous news for the over 400,000 low-income Hoosiers served by our consumer-driven program. __Indiana is a national model for how states can support better health outcomes while encouraging personal responsibility and reducing costs__. With this waiver from the federal government, we will also have an opportunity to expand critical addiction treatment options and our efforts to help Hoosiers find employment."
**-Indiana House Speaker Brian Bosma**

"__Rural Hoosiers and rural health care providers have benefited from the 400,000 residents who joined HIP__. The Indiana Rural Health Association is supportive of the new waiver, which provides additional and much needed services. Tobacco cessation and substance abuse treatment, which will now be covered services, offer needed opportunities to improve the overall health of rural populations.”
**-Don Kelso, Executive Director, Indiana Rural Health Association**

“The approved HIP waiver is __great news for Indiana and working, low-income Hoosiers__ who are trying to manage their health care needs and costs. The addition of tobacco cessation and substance use disorder incentives will have an even larger impact on improving the overall health of Indiana.”
**-Dennis M. Murphy, President and CEO, Indiana University Health **

"Improving access to care, and especially to preventive care and mental health services, is critically important to the health of our community. One of the best things we can do for the health of our patients is to ensure they are signed up for every resource available to them, with __HIP being a turning point for many Hoosier families__."
**-Lisa Harris, MD, CEO, Eskenazi Health **
---
|
---
| | _**Read More:**_

* **Inside Indiana Business: **[HIP Expansion Lands Approval](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=f1c8770eee&e=716de7d363)
* **WISH: **[Feds commit up to $80M annually in 3-year extension for Healthy Indiana Plan](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=6d85e7c997&e=716de7d363)

HIP has been a great success, providing health care for 400,000 low-income Hoosiers, and I’m pleased that this consumer-driven program will continue to operate here in Indiana." -Indiana Senate President Pro Tem David Long “This waiver extension of the Healthy Indiana Plan is tremendous news for the over 400,000 low-income Hoosiers served by our consumer-driven program. Indiana is a national model for how states can support better health outcomes while encouraging personal responsibility and reducing costs. With this waiver from the federal government, we will also have an opportunity to expand critical addiction treatment options and our efforts to help Hoosiers find employment." -Indiana House Speaker Brian Bosma "Rural Hoosiers and rural health care providers have benefited from the 400,000 residents who joined HIP. The Indiana Rural Health Association is supportive of the new waiver, which provides additional and much needed services. Tobacco cessation and substance abuse treatment, which will now be covered services, offer needed opportunities to improve the overall health of rural populations.” -Don Kelso, Executive Director, Indiana Rural Health Association “The approved HIP waiver is great news for Indiana and working, low-income Hoosiers who are trying to manage their health care needs and costs. The addition of tobacco cessation and substance use disorder incentives will have an even larger impact on improving the overall health of Indiana.” -Dennis M. Murphy, President and CEO, Indiana University Health "Improving access to care, and especially to preventive care and mental health services, is critically important to the health of our community. One of the best things we can do for the health of our patients is to ensure they are signed up for every resource available to them, with HIP being a turning point for many Hoosier families." -Lisa Harris, MD, CEO, Eskenazi Health | | | Read More: Official Press Release: Inside Indiana Business: WISH: | | | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Team Holcomb 101 W. name: Jay Inslee
subject: It's time to step up and pass a price on carbon pollution.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
A price on carbon is what we need to fight climate change in Washington. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb8/2f5ea88d/73ac7e6b/2cbc9ae8/2299295543/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/carbon-pricing") Friend, This is our time, Washington. **It's time to step up and pass a price on carbon pollution.** [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb8/2f5ea88d/73ac7e6b/2cbc9ae8/2299295543/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/carbon-pricing") Thursday night, our legislation to implement carbon pricing passed out of its first Senate committee. This is a common-sense plan to put a price on carbon pollution and join leaders across the globe in taking action. It is a critical step forward to combat climate change -- not just for Washington, but for the country. It's time for us to show that Democrats and Republicans in Washington can work together to take action on climate change, even when Washington, D.C. won't. A price on carbon pollution is a common-sense proposal and a _proven_ way to reduce our carbon footprint. It will have a tremendous impact on the state by encouraging Washington companies to make more sustainable choices. Carbon pricing will finance the development of clean energy jobs, expanding our economy and employment opportunities. It will spur innovation in building a high-tech, clean energy future. With a price on carbon pollution, we'll build the future we want in Washington, and the clean energy economy that comes with it. We know there will be jobs created in the next decade in the business of reducing carbon and fighting climate change. Do we want those jobs here or overseas? We know we can grow our thriving clean energy sector in Washington, and a price on carbon pollution is how we get there. It's what our state needs. With committee passage, momentum is on our side. We will be joining in action with the ten states that currently have carbon pricing or cap-and-trade measures to carve the path towards a more sustainable future. I want to be clear: Our efforts can be a model for the nation, which we desperately need in the vacuum of leadership left by the Trump administration. Action to stop carbon pollution is long overdue. I'm excited about this legislation, and I hope you are too. [**With our bill's passage out of its first committee, we need to seize this momentum. At this critical moment, I want to hear from you. Take a second to let me know what you think of our plan to put a price on carbon pollution.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb8/2f5ea88d/73ac7e6b/2cbc9ae8/2299295543/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/carbon-pricing") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [TAKE THE SURVEY »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb8/2f5ea88d/73ac7e6b/2cbc9ae8/2299295543/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/carbon-pricing")

name: Roy Cooper
subject: End gerrymandering now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Republican politicians have been choosing their constituents instead of constituents choosing their politicians.

|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203692/2fde7c66/47b0fe9d/1569eb90/3671802850/VEsH/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")
---
Something's not right here, friend.

Republican politicians have been choosing their constituents instead of constituents choosing their politicians.

Using new map-making software, Republican leaders in the North Carolina legislature and all across the country have rigged the system.

It's time to end this.

The majority of Americans want more balance in their legislatures and in Congress so more consensus can be built and more bad ideas stopped. Folks are standing up across North Carolina -- and across the country -- to demand an end to this rigged system.

[**I 'm standing with Pennsylvania Governor Tom Wolf, Missouri Senator Claire McCaskill, and a coalition of leaders and organizations across the country to restore our democracy and end gerrymandering. Will you join us?**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203692/2fde7c66/47b0fe9d/1569eb90/3671802850/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")

North Carolina is a purple state. We're split pretty evenly down the middle. But that's not how we're represented.

Using technologically diabolical gerrymandering, Republicans have drawn maps that give them supermajorities in both the State House and the State Senate.

Not only that, there are 10 Republican members of Congress representing North Carolina compared to just three who are Democrats.

That just doesn't add up -- and it's not just happening here. In states across the country, Republicans have created wildly contorted districts to gain victory for their party, regardless of what voters want.

[**If you're fed up with gerrymandering, add your name to the grassroots movement that's restoring our democracy.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1203692/2fde7c66/47b0fe9d/1569eb90/3671802850/VEsF/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")

Thanks for all you continue to do,
